items.title: Designing for Discovery: Shaping Imageomics Tools for Biologists
items.text:
A hands-on workshop at the first Imageomics Conference, hosted by the Imageomics Institute.

  • Dates: April 16 (1:00–5:00 PM) & April 17 (8:00 AM–1:00 PM), 2026
  • Location: Energy Advancement and Innovation Center, The Ohio State University, Columbus, OH
path: Designing for Discovery: Shaping Imageomics Tools for Biologists

items.title: About
items.text: The Imageomics Institute has built a powerful suite of open-source tools for image-based biological research. This workshop is dedicated to bridging the gap between these computational tools and biological discovery. By partnering directly with biologists across all career stages, we aim to pressure-test existing workflows, uncover novel applications, and ensure these tools are as intuitive as they are innovative.
path: Designing for Discovery: Shaping Imageomics Tools for Biologists

items.title: Tutorials
items.text:
Each tutorial walks through a specific Imageomics tool, from setup to hands-on use with example data.

Tutorial Description pybioclip Classify focal species and generate image embeddings programmatically without complex ML infrastructure. Embedding Explorer Explore image embeddings interactively—try different models, projections, and see which images cluster together or apart. BioCLIP Search Lite Given a query image, find the most visually similar organisms across 200M+ samples from the TreeofLife dataset. SST Segmentation Segment traits from specimen images and apply automatic landmarking. CAM Trait Extraction Explore fine-grained trait distinctions and discriminative traits between species using Prompt-CAM and Finer-CAM. SAE Discover what traits are common among organisms across a collection of images.
path: Designing for Discovery: Shaping Imageomics Tools for Biologists

items.title: Workshop Structure
items.text:
• Day 1 (Thursday, April 16): Live demonstrations and guided tutorials. Open to all Imageomics Conference participants.
  • Day 2 (Friday, April 17): Interactive, hands-on deep dive with a smaller group. Participants work with their own data and use cases.
path: Designing for Discovery: Shaping Imageomics Tools for Biologists

items.title: Prompt-CAM
items.text: Prompt-CAM helps you explore fine-grained trait distinctions between different specified species. Use it when you want to understand what visual features differentiate species you select.

items.title: Finer-CAM
items.text: Finer-CAM helps you better understand discriminative traits between similar species. Use it when you want to identify subtle differences that distinguish closely related organisms.
